question_text: What do all electromagnetic waves have in common?
correct_answer: They all travel at the same speed (the speed of light) in a vacuum, and they do not require a medium to travel through.
explanation: All electromagnetic waves, whether radio waves, microwaves, infrared, visible light, ultraviolet, X-rays, or gamma rays, share the same fundamental properties. They all travel at approximately 300,000,000 metres per second (the speed of light) when moving through a vacuum, and they can all travel through empty space without needing a material medium like air or water to carry them.
